name: Linearizer

consumes.minimum: 1 active comp + 1 target property with ≥2 keyframes + 1 driver property (1D scalar or multi-dimensional with dimension chosen)
consumes.description: Reads the active CompItem; reads all pre-selected properties at script start for batch mode (falls back to interactive single-property selection); reads keyframe values and times from each target property; reads the driver property value and its full expression path; reads the driver layer name to locate the checkbox effect; reads the driver property dimension when multi-dimensional.

controls.name: Select Target Property
controls.role: captures the currently selected property as the target (interactive mode only; skipped when properties are pre-selected at launch)

controls.name: Select Driver Property
controls.role: captures the currently selected property as the driver; for 2D/3D/4D properties opens a dimension-picker dialog

controls.name: Min Value
controls.role: sets the minimum driver value that maps to the first target keyframe

controls.name: Max Value
controls.role: sets the maximum driver value that maps to the last target keyframe

controls.name: Apply Linear Expression
controls.role: validates inputs then writes the linear expression onto every target property and closes the palette

controls.name: Cancel
controls.role: closes the palette without applying any expression

functions: guard: alerts and exits when no active composition is present
guard: alerts and exits when any pre-selected property has fewer than 2 keyframes
capture target properties from pre-selected comp properties (batch mode) or from interactive button click (single mode)
capture driver property from selected comp property; for multi-dimensional drivers prompt the user to pick a dimension (X/Y/Z/W)
determine property dimensionality (1D/2D/3D) by probing test expressions with zero side-effect restore
traverse property hierarchy to build the fully-qualified AE expression path for the driver property
traverse property hierarchy to find the parent group name for naming the checkbox controller
add an ADBE Checkbox Control effect to the driver layer and name it 'Uniform Interpolation || <target description>'
capture all keyframe values and times from each target property
compute relative keyframe time fractions for proportional segment distribution
generate a 1D linear expression using mapSliderToValue (scalar lerp with clamping)
generate a 2D linear expression using mapSliderToValue2D (per-component lerp with clamping)
generate a 3D linear expression using mapSliderToValue3D (per-component lerp with clamping)
generate a shape-path (SHAPE) expression using linearArrayInterpolation (per-vertex/tangent lerp)
write the generated expression string onto each target property
wrap all mutations in a single undo group named 'Linearizer'

scriptSim.scenario: success – 1D opacity linked to slider driver
scriptSim.inputs: Active comp 'Main Comp' (1920×1080, 24fps, 10s); driver layer 'Driver Null' with a Slider Control effect (value 50); target layer 'Target Shape' with Opacity keyed at t=0→0%, t=2→50%, t=4→100%; Opacity pre-selected (batch mode); user selects driver Slider, enters Min=0 Max=100, clicks Apply

sim.note: Sweeps the Slider Control value from 0 to 100 (minVal→maxVal); output is the linearly interpolated Opacity value (0%→50%→100%) using the 3-keyframe piecewise mapping with relative time distribution (t=0→t=2→t=4 maps to segments [0,50] and [50,100]).

notes: UI mode is declared DIALOG in @ui frontmatter but the live window is created with Window('palette') — the harness treats it as DIALOG per frontmatter.
Batch mode activates automatically when properties are pre-selected at script launch; single interactive mode is used otherwise.
The checkbox controller (ADBE Checkbox Control) is added to the driver layer and controls even-vs-relative interpolation from inside the generated expression.
Hold keyframes are not supported and will silently produce incorrect results.
3D/4D target properties are rejected with an alert at target-select time.
For multi-dimensional driver properties a secondary sub-dialog (Window 'dialog') prompts for X/Y/Z/W dimension selection; this sub-dialog is not exposed as a top-level control here.
Expressions are generated as ECMA 3-compatible strings using a custom serializeForExpression serializer (no JSON.stringify).
Shape path (SHAPE) targets produce a different expression body using linearArrayInterpolation over vertex/tangent arrays; the fixture success scenario covers the 1D (Opacity) case only.
